top_stories.title: Okta expands Cross App Access ecosystem to secure AI agent connections
top_stories.summary: Okta announced that more than 25 software vendors have joined its Cross App Access ecosystem, which governs how AI agents connect to enterprise applications through identity infrastructure. The framework routes AI agent connections through a company's identity layer, extending IAM controls to agentic AI workflows.

top_stories.title: Omada Identity Sovereign targets Europe’s growing digital sovereignty demands
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched Omada Identity Sovereign, a new solution designed to give organizations direct control over the deployment location and management of their identity governance infrastructure. The product targets European digital sovereignty requirements, addressing data residency, operational control, and related compliance demands.

top_stories.title: Omada Introduces Omada Identity Sovereign, Bringing Digital Sovereignty for Regulated Organizations
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched Omada Identity Sovereign, a new solution designed for regulated European organizations to deploy and operate identity governance on infrastructure of their own choosing. The product ensures no non-European entity is involved in the chain, addressing digital sovereignty requirements for compliance-driven organizations.

top_stories.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
top_stories.summary: A large-scale credential-harvesting operation dubbed "FortiBleed," attributed to a Russian-speaking initial access broker, targeted over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls and collected 110 million credentials. The campaign, active since February 2026, involved credential list collection, exposed service scanning, brute-forcing, and custom tooling against FortiGate infrastructure.

top_stories.title: Navigating the hidden costs and governance risks of Microsoft 365 E7
top_stories.summary: Microsoft launched the Microsoft 365 E7 (Frontier Suite) in May 2026 at $99 per user per month, bundling E5 capabilities with Microsoft 365 Copilot and the Entra Suite for advanced identity features. The article examines the hidden costs and governance risks associated with this new licensing tier, which directly incorporates Microsoft Entra identity and access management components.

news.title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
news.summary: Polish authorities arrested four members of a cybercrime group that conducted SIM-swapping attacks by breaching telecommunications partners and hijacking email accounts. The attacks resulted in millions in cryptocurrency theft, exploiting weaknesses in SMS-based authentication methods.

news.title: Webinar: Why account takeovers remain one of the hardest threats to stop
news.summary: A webinar addresses the persistent challenge of account takeover attacks, which are difficult to detect because attackers operate through legitimate accounts and trusted services. The session focuses on using behavioral AI to identify compromised accounts and automate response workflows.

news.title: Reco Agent Security helps organizations govern AI agents and reduce exposure
news.summary: Reco launched Reco Agent Security, an expansion of the Reco Platform designed to govern AI agents operating across enterprise applications and workflows. The product addresses security risks related to AI agents accessing sensitive data, invoking tools, triggering workflows, and taking autonomous actions across connected systems.

news.title: Mitiga unveils Agentic Runtime Security for cloud, SaaS, identity, and AI protection
news.summary: Mitiga has launched Agentic Runtime Security, a detection and response platform covering cloud, SaaS, identity, AI, and third-party services. The product includes identity as one of several protected surfaces, positioning itself as a successor to endpoint-centric security operations.

news.title: Web社内報サービスSOLANOWA、IdP連携の新オプション「ユーザー自動同期（SCIM）」を提供開始
news.summary: Japanese web intranet service SOLANOWA has launched a new "User Auto Sync (SCIM)" option for IdP integration. The feature enables automatic user synchronization between identity providers and the SOLANOWA platform using the SCIM protocol.

news.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
news.summary: A coordinated law enforcement operation involving Europol and private sector partners including Microsoft disrupted the Amadey and StealC malware network. The operation recovered 27 million stolen credentials, targeting infrastructure used to facilitate ransomware, financial fraud, and attacks on critical infrastructure.

news.title: Restrict AWS Management Console access to expected networks with sign-in resource-based policies and RCPs
news.summary: AWS announced support for resource-based policies and resource control policies (RCPs) for AWS Sign-In, enabling restrictions on AWS Management Console access to specific expected networks. The feature allows administrators to control console sign-in and CLI access through policy-based network restrictions, directly addressing access management and authorization controls for cloud environments.

news.title: Show HN: Lelu – gate OpenAI agent actions on confidence and prompt injection
news.summary: An open source project called Lelu has been released as an authorization engine for AI agents, featuring confidence-aware gating, policy-as-code, and audit trail capabilities. The tool is designed to control and gate OpenAI agent actions, incorporating human-in-the-loop review and prompt injection protection.

news.title: SOFTwarfare Launches on Google Cloud Marketplace, Expands Public Sector Reach With Carahsoft
news.summary: SOFTwarfare has launched its identity defense solution on the Google Cloud Marketplace, partnering with Carahsoft to expand access to public sector customers. The go-to-market expansion aims to make the company's continuous identity defense capabilities available to both commercial and government clients.

news.title: Securing the service desk: Why social engineering attacks keep succeeding
news.summary: Service desks are being targeted by social engineering attacks aimed at obtaining password resets, MFA changes, and corporate account access. Specops Software outlines the mechanics of these attacks and provides defensive recommendations for organizations.

news.title: Flare Leads on Identity-First Threat Intelligence with Platform Expansions
news.summary: Flare announced platform expansions focused on identity-first threat intelligence capabilities. The new features aim to help security teams investigate threats and reduce identity risk from a single platform.

news.title: Securing local administrator accounts with Windows LAPS and Microsoft Intune
news.summary: Microsoft's Windows Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) is now natively integrated into the Windows operating system, enabling automatic rotation of unique passwords for local administrator accounts with escrow to a directory service. The article covers how IT administrators can manage LAPS through Microsoft Intune to secure local accounts, a topic directly relevant to privileged access management (PAM).

news.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
news.summary: Attackers breached application vendor Klue and used its stolen OAuth tokens to access and exfiltrate Salesforce customer data, with the scope of victims expanding. The incident highlights OAuth token compromise as an attack vector affecting third-party application integrations with Salesforce platforms.

news.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
news.summary: Threat actors deployed a Golang-based sniffer targeting 430,000 FortiGate firewalls to harvest credentials in an ongoing global campaign. The attack identified approximately 110 million credentials by engineering the compromised firewalls to act as credential-stealing tools.

news.title: Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
news.summary: President Trump signed Executive Order 14409 on June 22, requiring federal agencies to migrate high-value assets and high-impact systems to post-quantum cryptography by December 31, 2030, with digital signatures required to migrate by December 31, 2031. National security systems are placed on a separate migration track under the order.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise enterprise systems.

news.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of SailPoint, Inc. - SAIL
news.summary: Pomerantz Law Firm has announced an investigation into claims on behalf of SailPoint, Inc. (NASDAQ: SAIL) investors. The investigation is a legal/financial matter concerning investor claims rather than a product, security, or operational development at the IAM vendor.

news.title: Microsoft Entra ID single sign-on for the Denodo connector in Power BI (Preview)
news.summary: Microsoft has released a preview feature enabling Microsoft Entra ID single sign-on for the Denodo connector in Power BI. Users querying semantic models in DirectQuery mode are authenticated to Denodo using their own Entra ID identity, removing on-premises dependencies.

news.title: Mastering Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: A book titled "Mastering Microsoft Entra ID" is scheduled for publication on June 26, 2026, covering design, implementation, security, and governance of identity solutions in hybrid enterprise environments. The 678-page paperback is published under ISBN 978-1806116010.

news.title: The cybersecurity industry built a $200B business selling you problems. Nobody got paid to fix them.
news.summary: The article argues that the cybersecurity industry has prioritized risk detection over remediation, creating a $200B market built around identifying problems rather than solving them. It references IAM-relevant issues such as dormant user accounts and excessive privileges as examples of unresolved risk categories.

news.title: He Thought He Was Secure; His Phone Number Was Stolen Anyway
news.summary: The article covers SIM swap attacks as a method for stealing one-time passwords (OTPs) delivered via SMS, enabling account takeovers. The piece highlights the vulnerability of SMS-based MFA and advocates for stronger, layered authentication measures.

news.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
news.summary: A 29-year-old heap over-read vulnerability in the Squid web proxy, dubbed "Squidbleed," can expose cleartext HTTP requests including credentials and session tokens to other users sharing the same proxy. The flaw originates from a 1997 FTP-parsing change and remains present in Squid's default configuration, with disclosure by Calif.io researchers in June.

news.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Highlights Software Development Pipeline Risk
news.summary: A breach at Novo Nordisk involved a leaked GitHub token that exposed vulnerabilities in software development pipeline security. The incident centers on secrets management being framed as an identity problem rather than a tooling problem, highlighting gaps in how organizations handle non-human identity and credential governance.

news.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
news.summary: A security vulnerability in FIFA's systems allowed potential remote takeover of World Cup video streams due to unenforced Microsoft Entra access controls. The bug demonstrated how misconfigured identity and access management policies could enable unauthorized control over broadcast infrastructure.

news.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
news.summary: A large-scale credential-harvesting campaign has compromised over 30,000 Fortinet devices across nearly 200 countries. Attackers have compiled a list of working credentials from the compromised devices, actively targeting organizations across various sectors.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords, briefly compromising high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI chatbot into performing unauthorized password resets were circulated on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Congressional lawmakers in both chambers are demanding answers from CISA while the agency works to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked credentials provided access to several privileged AWS GovCloud accounts along with files detailing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes.
